campaign_brief.objective: Establish LSAD as the default low-SWaP autonomous detection platform for forward-deployed ISR and base-defense missions, securing 3-5 program-of-record evaluations and 2 OTA prototype contracts within the FY2026 budget cycle.
campaign_brief.target_audience: DoD program managers, PEO/PM staff, and prime integrator capture leads responsible for counter-UAS, base perimeter security, and contested-environment ISR procurement.
campaign_brief.key_message: LSAD delivers ATR-grade autonomous detection in a battery-powered package that survives where GPS and comms fail.
campaign_brief.proof_point: Demonstrated 94% detection accuracy across 12-hour autonomous missions in GPS-denied field trials at 8W average power draw, validated against a documented Army Test & Evaluation Command protocol.
campaign_brief.call_to_action: Schedule a classified-capable capability briefing and request an LSAD evaluation kit for your next field exercise.

primary_persona.role: DoD Program Manager / PEO Capability Lead (O-5/O-6 or GS-14/15 equivalent)
pain_points: Existing sensor suites fail in GPS-denied and comms-degraded contested environments
Power and logistics tail of legacy systems strains forward-deployed sustainment
Pressure to field counter-UAS and base-defense capability faster than the traditional acquisition cycle allows
Risk-averse acquisition culture penalizes unproven vendors lacking TRL evidence and security accreditation
information_sources: Defense News, Breaking Defense, and C4ISRNET
AUSA, SOFIC, and Sea-Air-Space conference floors and panels
SBIR/STTR and OTA solicitation portals (SAM.gov, DSIP)
Peer program managers and prime integrator capture teams

buying_committee.their_question: Does this reduce my program risk and fit my fielding timeline?
buying_committee.content_needed: TRL evidence package, fielding roadmap, and OTA/SBIR pathway one-pager

buying_committee.their_question: Is this contractually clean and compliant with FAR/DFARS and CMMC?
buying_committee.content_needed: Compliance matrix covering CMMC 2.0, ITAR, and supply-chain (NDAA 889) attestations

buying_committee.their_question: Will it actually perform in our threat environment and integrate with our C2 stack?
buying_committee.content_needed: Detailed performance datasheet, API/ICD documentation, and field-trial test report

buying_committee.their_question: Can I teach this into my proposal to differentiate my bid?
buying_committee.content_needed: Teaming brief, past-performance summary, and capability-gap mapping to active solicitations

buying_committee.their_question: Does this close a validated capability gap worth funding?
buying_committee.content_needed: Capability-gap white paper aligned to JCIDS and combatant command priorities

icp.company_size: Prime integrators (1,000+ employees) and DoD program offices managing $10M-$500M acquisition portfolios
geographies: United States (DoD CONUS program offices and labs)
Five Eyes partner nations (UK, Australia, Canada)

messaging_hierarchy.headline: Detection That Survives the Contested Edge
messaging_hierarchy.subheadline: LSAD brings autonomous, low-power ATR to GPS-denied missions where legacy sensors go dark.
messaging_hierarchy.body_message: When comms are jammed and GPS is spoofed, LSAD keeps watching. Engineered for forward-deployed ISR and base defense, LSAD runs 12-hour autonomous detection missions on battery power, classifies threats onboard without a backhaul link, and integrates into existing C2 architectures through open interfaces. It shortens your fielding timeline through proven OTA and SBIR pathways while meeting CMMC, ITAR, and NDAA supply-chain requirements.
messaging_hierarchy.tone: Authoritative, technically credible, mission-focused, and free of hype

channels.rationale: Face-to-face capability demos and booth presence are where DoD program managers and prime capture leads evaluate hardware and initiate relationships.
channels.kpi: Qualified booth demos booked with PM/capture-level attendees

channels.channel: Targeted account-based marketing (ABM) to program offices and primes
channels.rationale: The buying universe is small and named; precision outreach to specific PEOs and capture teams outperforms broad demand-gen in defense.
channels.kpi: Capability briefings scheduled with named target accounts

channels.channel: Defense trade media + thought leadership (Breaking Defense, C4ISRNET)
channels.rationale: Earned and sponsored content builds the third-party credibility and air-cover that risk-averse acquisition stakeholders require before engaging.
channels.kpi: Sponsored briefing engagement and inbound demo requests

channels.channel: SBIR/STTR + OTA solicitation pursuit and white papers
channels.rationale: Government innovation pathways are the fastest contractual on-ramp for a deep-tech entrant; proactive white papers seed program-of-record interest.
channels.kpi: White papers submitted and downselect/award invitations received

channel_recommendation.channel_sequence: Lead with SBIR/OTA white papers and trade-media thought leadership 4-5 months pre-launch to build credibility, activate ABM outreach 8 weeks out to book briefings, then concentrate conference demos around the launch window and FY budget-formulation cycle.

allocation.channel: Targeted account-based marketing (ABM) to program offices and primes
Defense trade media + thought leadership (Breaking Defense, C4ISRNET)
SBIR/STTR + OTA solicitation pursuit and white papers

content_creative_plan.hero_asset: A 3-minute field-trial demonstration video showing LSAD autonomously detecting and classifying threats in a GPS-denied, comms-degraded environment, with overlaid performance telemetry (detection accuracy, power draw, mission duration).

supporting_assets.purpose: Arm technical SMEs and systems engineers with verifiable performance specs and integration interfaces
supporting_assets.specs: 2-page PDF, classification-cleared for public release, includes ICD/API summary and SWaP table

supporting_assets.purpose: Pre-answer contracting officer and security questions on CMMC, ITAR, and NDAA 889
supporting_assets.specs: 1-page PDF, columns mapping each requirement to current status/evidence

supporting_assets.purpose: Map LSAD to validated JCIDS gaps and seed requirements-officer interest for OTA/SBIR pursuit
supporting_assets.specs: 6-8 pages, references combatant command priorities, distribution Statement A

supporting_assets.purpose: Enable live, hands-on detection demonstrations at booth and in private briefings
supporting_assets.specs: Ruggedized transport case, running unit, tablet dashboard, scripted 8-minute demo flow

supporting_assets.purpose: Open conversations with named program managers and capture leads
supporting_assets.specs: 5-touch sequence, role-tailored value props, links to gated technical brief

content_calendar.action: Publish capability-gap white paper and begin trade-media thought-leadership placements
Release technical datasheet and compliance matrix on gated landing page
Launch ABM email/LinkedIn sequences to top 50 named accounts
Premiere hero field-trial video and amplify across LinkedIn and trade media
Conference demo activation at primary trade show; book private briefings
Publish field-trial test-report summary and customer-evaluation testimonials
Submit SBIR/OTA white papers aligned to open solicitations; nurture warm briefings

actions: Complete and clear field-trial test report and hero video for public release
Publish capability-gap white paper and seed trade-media thought leadership
Build target account list of named program offices and prime capture teams
Finalize CMMC/ITAR/NDAA compliance matrix and demo kit
Premiere hero field-trial video and issue coordinated trade-media announcement
Activate conference booth demos and pre-booked private capability briefings
Launch full ABM sequence to all named target accounts
Open evaluation-kit request and briefing-scheduling pipeline
Convert briefings into field-evaluation kits and document results
Submit SBIR/STTR and OTA white papers to open solicitations
Nurture buying committee with role-specific assets and follow-up demos
Publish evaluation testimonials and refine messaging from win/loss feedback

launch_timeline.defense_timing_note: Align launch to the US Government fiscal year: FY2026 begins 1 October 2025. Concentrate white-paper submissions and capability briefings in the spring-summer 2025 POM/budget-formulation window so LSAD is reflected in FY2026 program planning, and prepare for the typical Q4 (July-September) end-of-fiscal-year procurement surge when unobligated funds are committed. Account for the recurring risk of continuing resolutions, which can freeze new-start funding into early calendar 2026.

sales_enablement.one_pager_summary: LSAD is a low-SWaP autonomous detection platform that delivers ATR-grade threat classification in GPS-denied, comms-degraded environments. Field-validated at 94% detection accuracy across 12-hour battery-powered missions at 8W, it integrates into existing C2 architectures via open interfaces and meets CMMC, ITAR, and NDAA supply-chain requirements. Available through SBIR/STTR and OTA pathways for accelerated fielding in FY2026.
talk_track: Open with the mission problem: 'When your sensors lose GPS and comms in a contested fight, what keeps watching the perimeter?'
Introduce LSAD as autonomous, low-power detection that classifies threats onboard with no backhaul dependency.
Anchor credibility with the field-trial proof point: 94% accuracy, 12-hour autonomy, 8W average draw, validated to ATEC protocol.
Connect to their acquisition reality: open architecture for C2 integration, full compliance posture, and SBIR/OTA pathways that fit the FY2026 timeline.
Close with a low-risk next step: request an evaluation kit for the next field exercise and book a classified-capable deep-dive briefing.

objection_handling.objection: You're an unproven vendor — what's your past performance and TRL?
objection_handling.response: LSAD is at TRL 7 with documented field trials against an ATEC-aligned protocol; we offer a no-cost evaluation kit so your engineers verify performance in your own environment before any commitment, and we pursue SBIR/OTA pathways specifically structured to de-risk early adoption.

objection_handling.objection: How does this integrate with our existing C2 and sensor architecture?
objection_handling.response: LSAD exposes an open, documented ICD and API and outputs standards-based message formats; we provide the integration guide upfront and can run a joint integration spike during evaluation to prove compatibility with your stack.

objection_handling.objection: Can you meet our security and supply-chain compliance requirements?
objection_handling.response: We maintain a current compliance matrix covering CMMC 2.0, ITAR registration, and NDAA Section 889 supply-chain attestations, and our hardware BOM is sourced from vetted, allied suppliers — we'll share the full evidence package with your contracting officer.

objection_handling.objection: We don't have funding or a contract vehicle this cycle.
objection_handling.response: That's exactly why we lead with SBIR/STTR and OTA prototype pathways, which provide non-traditional funding on-ramps outside the standard PPBE cycle; we'll help map LSAD to an open solicitation and co-author the white paper.

demo_sequence: Set the scenario: stage LSAD in a simulated GPS-denied, comms-degraded forward position
Trigger a representative threat (UAS or ground intrusion) and show real-time onboard detection and classification
Display the live dashboard telemetry — detection confidence, power draw, mission-clock autonomy
Demonstrate output handoff into a mock C2 interface to prove integration
Conclude with the evaluation-kit offer and a tailored fielding-pathway discussion

kpis.primary_kpi: Number of LSAD field evaluations and OTA/SBIR prototype awards initiated within the FY2026 cycle (target: 5 evaluations, 2 awards)

channel_kpis.kpi: Qualified booth/private demos with PM or capture-level attendees
channel_kpis.target: 40 qualified demos across the conference season

channel_kpis.channel: Targeted account-based marketing (ABM) to program offices and primes
channel_kpis.kpi: Capability briefings scheduled with named target accounts
channel_kpis.target: 25 briefings from the top-50 target list

channel_kpis.channel: Defense trade media + thought leadership (Breaking Defense, C4ISRNET)
channel_kpis.kpi: Inbound demo/briefing requests attributable to media content

channel_kpis.channel: SBIR/STTR + OTA solicitation pursuit and white papers
channel_kpis.kpi: White papers submitted and downselect invitations received

kpis.reporting_cadence: Weekly pipeline standup with sales; monthly executive dashboard; quarterly QBR aligned to FY budget milestones

risks.risk: A continuing resolution freezes FY2026 new-start funding, delaying program awards
risks.mitigation: Prioritize SBIR/STTR and OTA pathways that fund outside standard PPBE timing, and use the delay window to deepen field evaluations so LSAD is shovel-ready when funds release

risks.risk: Long acquisition cycles stall pipeline conversion beyond the campaign window
risks.mitigation: Front-load no-cost evaluation kits and prototype OTAs to create contractual momentum early, and structure ABM nurture to sustain buying-committee engagement across multi-quarter cycles

risks.risk: Failure to secure security accreditation or compliance attestations blocks engagement with risk-averse buyers
risks.mitigation: Treat CMMC/ITAR/NDAA evidence as a launch gate; complete the compliance matrix before outreach and assign a dedicated owner to maintain attestations current

risks.risk: Field-trial performance fails to replicate in a customer's specific threat environment
risks.mitigation: Run joint, customer-defined evaluation protocols rather than vendor-controlled demos, and set transparent performance expectations to protect credibility

pivot_triggers: Fewer than 10 qualified briefings booked 8 weeks after ABM launch — reallocate budget toward conference demos and direct PM outreach
Continuing resolution extends past Q1 CY2026 — shift emphasis fully to SBIR/OTA non-traditional funding pursuits
Trade-media content yields under 10 attributable inbounds by week 12 — cut sponsored media spend and redirect to ABM
Evaluation-to-award conversion stalls below 20% — convene win/loss review and adjust messaging or pricing model
